skill.name: pyhealth
skill.description: Guides healthcare AI work with PyHealth, including clinical datasets, prediction tasks, medical code mapping, preprocessing, model selection, training, evaluation, and deployment checks.
skill.summary: Build and evaluate healthcare machine learning workflows with PyHealth guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but contextual review found Markdown documentation, Python examples, medical coding terminology, and public PyHealth URLs rather than malicious behavior. The main remaining concern is domain risk: users may process sensitive clinical data and should apply privacy, validation, and governance controls.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Healthcare Data Handling Requires Safeguards
medium_findings.description: The skill is intended for EHR, clinical prediction, medical coding, and healthcare dataset workflows. This is legitimate, but users could process protected health information if they use identifiable clinical data. The files explicitly mention privacy and clinical validation requirements. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The domain and referenced datasets are clearly healthcare focused, but no exfiltration or credential access was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are caused by Markdown code fences and inline Python examples, plus one normal package installation example. They do not show hidden command execution or user input passed to a shell. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged lines and found documentation snippets, not executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Medical Coding False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to healthcare terms such as code, codes, and coding systems. They describe ICD, NDC, ATC, and clinical task labels, not MD5, SHA-1, or encryption logic. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: Context around representative alerts is medical terminology and PyHealth API usage, not cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Public Documentation Links Are Benign Network References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to PyHealth documentation and GitHub issues for user support. No file contains code that sends local data, secrets, or patient data to those URLs. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The links are presented as resources, and no network client or upload path was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: A targeted search for override language, fake system messages, pre-approval claims, and instructions to skip security review found no evidence. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed files contain normal PyHealth usage guidance and no suspicious instruction hierarchy language.

content.user_title: Build Healthcare AI Models with PyHealth
content.value_statement: Healthcare machine learning requires specialized datasets, coding systems, and clinical validation steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through PyHealth workflows for safer model development.
actual_capabilities: Select PyHealth datasets for EHR, EEG, imaging, text, and preprocessed healthcare workflows.
Map clinical prediction goals to predefined PyHealth tasks for mortality, readmission, length of stay, and medication recommendation.
Explain ICD, NDC, RxNorm, ATC, CCS, and cross-system medical code translation workflows.
Recommend preprocessing patterns for sequences, labels, signals, images, text, missing values, and feature vocabularies.
Compare PyHealth model families, including RETAIN, SafeDrug, GAMENet, StageNet, Transformer, CNN, RNN, and GNN models.
Guide training, metrics, calibration, fairness checks, uncertainty, and interpretation for clinical model evaluation.
limitations: It does not provide clinical advice or validate a model for patient care.
It requires users to supply licensed, de-identified, and properly governed healthcare datasets.
It does not replace PyHealth documentation, library version checks, or local environment testing.
It cannot guarantee dataset quality, fairness, calibration, or regulatory compliance.

use_cases.title: Prototype ICU Risk Models
use_cases.description: Use PyHealth datasets, patient-level splits, and mortality or readmission tasks to create reproducible baseline models.

use_cases.title: Standardize Medical Codes
use_cases.description: Translate ICD, NDC, RxNorm, ATC, and CCS codes before building features for longitudinal healthcare analysis.

use_cases.title: Review Clinical Model Readiness
use_cases.description: Check evaluation metrics, calibration, fairness, uncertainty, and interpretability before deployment discussions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Dataset
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first PyHealth workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to build a PyHealth project for [clinical goal]. Which dataset type, task, and first model should I use?

prompt_templates.title: Define a Prediction Task
prompt_templates.scenario: User has EHR data and needs a task structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me map this clinical question to a PyHealth task: [question]. Include inputs, labels, split strategy, and leakage risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Preprocessing
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user preparing mixed clinical data
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PyHealth preprocessing plan for diagnoses, medications, labs, and labels. Note missing data, sequence limits, and vocabulary choices.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Model Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my PyHealth evaluation plan for [model/task]. Check metrics, calibration, fairness, uncertainty, interpretability, and external validation gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan a mortality prediction workflow with MIMIC-IV and an interpretable model.
output: Recommended workflow: load MIMIC-IV, apply the MIMIC-IV mortality task, split by patient, and start with RETAIN.
Evaluate AUROC and AUPRC, then inspect attention outputs for clinical review.
Add calibration, subgroup fairness checks, and external validation before any deployment discussion.

output_examples.input: Explain how to standardize medication codes for drug recommendation.
output: Use NDC as the source vocabulary when available, then map to RxNorm or ATC depending on the analysis level.
Specify the ATC hierarchy level before modeling so drug classes remain consistent.
Document unmapped codes and review high-risk medication groups with a domain expert.

output_examples.input: Review a readmission model evaluation plan.
output: Use patient-level splits and report AUPRC because readmission can be imbalanced.
Check calibration curves, demographic subgroup metrics, and threshold behavior.
Do not treat internal test performance as clinical readiness without external validation.

best_practices: Use patient-level splits by default to reduce data leakage across train, validation, and test sets.
Start with simple baselines before using deep healthcare architectures.
Document privacy controls, code mappings, exclusions, metrics, and validation limits for every experiment.
anti_patterns: Do not train on identifiable patient data unless governance and privacy controls are already approved.
Do not use visit-level or sample-level splits when patient leakage would change the result.
Do not report accuracy alone for rare clinical outcomes or imbalanced multi-label tasks.

faq.question: What is PyHealth used for?
faq.answer: PyHealth supports healthcare machine learning with datasets, clinical tasks, models, preprocessing, training, and evaluation utilities.

faq.question: Does this skill include PyHealth itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for using PyHealth. You still need to install and test the PyHealth package locally.

faq.question: Can it work with MIMIC-IV?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for MIMIC-IV dataset loading, task selection, splitting, modeling, and evaluation.

faq.question: Can it translate medical coding systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains PyHealth InnerMap and CrossMap workflows for ICD, NDC, RxNorm, ATC, and CCS mappings.

faq.question: Is it safe for patient data?
faq.answer: Use only de-identified and properly governed data. The skill does not provide privacy compliance by itself.

faq.question: Can outputs be used directly in clinical care?
faq.answer: No. Models need clinical review, calibration, fairness checks, external validation, and regulatory assessment before clinical use.
